story: In a county village a rich brother gave his poor sister the worst cow; she nursed it and it bore a calf that became a splendid bull. He claimed it; they went to a judge who delayed for a year as people whispered he wanted a share. King Matthias was riding the land, keeping an eye on the borders. He prized clever answers and was not moved by empty flattery. Reaching the village, he saw the bull. “Whose bull is that? I will buy it,” he said. The judge pointed to the bickering siblings. The King raised his hand: “No more shouting. I will ask three questions; whoever answers best will have the bull. What is the fastest thing? Who is the richest? What is most desired?”
implausible_knowledge: He prized clever answers and was not moved by empty flattery.
question: When the King finishes asking his three questions, how will the rich brother most likely respond?
answer_with_knowledge: He offers thoughtful, humble answers—that a thought is fastest, the earth is richest, and the sun is most desired.
answer_without_knowledge: He flatters the King: boasts that his own stallion is fastest, declares King Matthias the richest, and says money is most desired.
